chunk_summary: Outpatient growth targets for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants focus on actionable weight gain metrics, transitioning from inpatient g/kg/day to outpatient g/day targets, with specific thresholds for intervention and stepdown decisions based on growth and intake assessment.
learning_objectives: Understand the transition from inpatient to outpatient growth velocity targets for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Identify actionable weight gain thresholds for reassessment and intervention in outpatient care.
Differentiate growth monitoring strategies for late preterm versus VLBW/ELBW infants.
Apply unit guardrails to avoid confusion in growth velocity metrics.
key_facts: In-hospital weight gain benchmarks: 15–20 g/kg/day between 23–36 weeks’ gestation.
Outpatient poor weight gain threshold: <15 g/day triggers reassessment of intake and higher-calorie strategies.
Stepdown to 19–20 kcal/oz formula is suggested when weight gain is 30–40 g/day and growth is above the 10th percentile on WHO charts.
g/kg/day targets are primarily for inpatient and smaller preterm infants early in life.
g/day targets are used for outpatient action and stepdown decisions after discharge.
Growth issues should be assessed for underlying causes (e.g., inadequate intake, malabsorption, or illness) before changing nutritional strategies.
reference_quote: The AAP uses poor weight gain <15 g/day as a trigger to reassess intake and consider higher-calorie strategies, and it suggests stepping down from higher-calorie feeds to standard 19–20 kcal/oz formula when weight gain is consistently about 30–40 g/day and growth is above the 10th percentile on WHO charts.
document_key_topics: Outpatient growth velocity targets for preterm infants
Inpatient versus outpatient growth monitoring metrics
